tasks.title: Fix path traversal vulnerability in auth import/export
evidence: v1/auth/import and v1/auth/export take a raw 'path' from the request body
This allows reading/writing files anywhere on the disk the process has access to
plan: Modify handleAuthImport and handleAuthExport to validate the path
Restrict paths to a safe directory or use a predefined filename relative to DataDir
Consider removing absolute path support from these endpoints
notes: Fixed path traversal vulnerability in auth import/export
Added validateVaultPath to restrict paths to simple filenames
Paths now resolved relative to DATA_DIR only
Added comprehensive test coverage for path traversal attacks
Updated OpenAPI spec with path validation pattern

tasks.title: Implement RenderWaitModeNetworkIdle in ChromedpFetcher
evidence: ChromedpFetcher.performWait for RenderWaitModeNetworkIdle is a stub (2s sleep)
Robust network idle requires implementing event listeners for network.EventRequestPaused or similar
plan: Implement network activity tracking using chromedp listeners
Replace 2s sleep with a wait loop that checks for zero inflight requests
notes: Implemented network idle detection using chromedp event listeners
Replaced 2-second sleep stub with proper wait loop checking for zero inflight requests
Added comprehensive unit tests (7 test cases) and integration test
All tests pass including CI gate (generate, format, type-check, lint, build, test)
Fixed potential race conditions with atomic operations and channel double-close protection

tasks.title: Implement browser availability check
evidence: fetch.CheckBrowserAvailability is a stub returning nil
healthz endpoint relies on this to report browser status
plan: Implement actual checks for chrome/chromium on PATH
Implement check for playwright drivers if usePlaywright is true
notes: Implemented browser availability check with Chrome/Chromium detection on PATH and platform-specific paths
Added Playwright driver availability check with 10-second timeout protection
Made runtime.GOOS and os.Getenv testable via package-level variables
Comprehensive test coverage for all platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows)
All tests pass and CI gate passes

tasks.title: Capture actual status in ChromedpFetcher
evidence: ChromedpFetcher returns a hardcoded 200 status for all successful captures
It should capture the status of the main navigation response
plan: Use network.EventResponseReceived to capture the status of the main frame request
Update Result.Status with the actual captured status
notes: Implemented responseTracker using network.EventResponseReceived to capture actual HTTP status
Added robust url parsing with net/url for RFC-compliant URL matching
Fallback to 200 with Warn-level log if status not captured (backward compatibility)
18 comprehensive tests covering redirects, ports, query strings, edge cases
Thread-safe concurrent access protection with mutex

tasks.title: Optimize PlaywrightFetcher browser reuse
evidence: PlaywrightFetcher initializes a new Playwright and browser instance for every fetch
This is highly inefficient for concurrent or sequential scrapes
plan: Implement a singleton or pool for Playwright and browser instances
Ensure proper lifecycle management and cleanup
notes: Implemented singleton pattern for Playwright/Browser instances
Browser reused across fetch operations with isolated per-fetch contexts
Added comprehensive test coverage (7 tests including race detection)
Concurrent safety verified with -race flag
Refinement phase: context propagation, cleanup logging, sequential reuse test

tasks.title: Implement pagination in Store.List
evidence: Store.List returns all jobs in a single query
This will scale poorly as the number of jobs increases
plan: Add limit and offset parameters to Store.List
Update API and CLI to support pagination
notes: Added ListOptions with Limit/Offset and Defaults() method
Store.List now delegates to ListOpts for backward compatibility
API /v1/jobs accepts limit (default 100, max 1000) and offset (default 0) query params
CLI 'jobs list' accepts --limit and --offset flags
Bug fix: Store.List now returns rows.Err() instead of nil
Comprehensive test coverage for pagination, defaults, and edge cases

tasks.title: Implement job recovery on Manager startup
evidence: Manager.Start does not load existing 'Queued' jobs from the store
If the server restarts, pending jobs are never processed
plan: In Manager.Start, query the store for jobs with 'Queued' status
Enqueue these jobs into the worker pool
notes: Implemented job recovery on Manager startup with pagination support
Added Store.ListByStatus with pagination options (limit/offset)
Added recoverQueuedJobs method called in Manager.Start
Added comprehensive tests for recovery and pagination
CI gate passes all checks

tasks.title: Unify API error format to JSON
evidence: API error responses use plain text http.Error
The rest of the API returns JSON, creating inconsistency for clients
plan: Create a standard ErrorResponse struct
Refactor error handling to return JSON with appropriate status codes
notes: Added ErrorResponse struct with JSON error format
Added writeJSONError helper function
Replaced 54+ http.Error calls with writeJSONError
Updated OpenAPI spec with ErrorResponse schema
Added comprehensive test coverage for JSON error responses
All API endpoints now return consistent JSON errors

tasks.title: Fix runJobs cancel CLI to be server-aware
evidence: runJobs cancel creates a new Manager instance to cancel a job
This is incorrect if another manager (server) is already running and owns the job
plan: Modify cli.runJobs cancel to check if the API server is reachable
If server is running, use API call to cancel; otherwise fall back to local store update
notes: Fixed cancel CLI to detect and use API when server is running
Removed incorrect Manager instantiation that conflicted with server-owned jobs
Added isServerRunning() helper with 2s health check timeout
Added cancelJobViaAPI() helper with 5s timeout and ErrorResponse parsing
CI passed: all tests green including e2e (22.3s)

tasks.title: Support SchemaInteger in validation
evidence: extract.validateScalar only handles SchemaNumber
SchemaInteger is missing as a explicit type even though it's common in schemas
plan: Add SchemaInteger to SchemaType enum
Update validateScalar to handle integer validation (no decimals allowed)
notes: Add SchemaInteger constant to SchemaType enum
Implement integer validation with math.Trunc for decimal detection
Extract validateNumericConstraints helper to eliminate duplication with SchemaNumber
Add 10 new test cases covering valid/invalid integers, boundaries, and scientific notation
Edge case: Inf without max constraint would be accepted (same as SchemaNumber)

tasks.title: Deep nesting support in Schema validation
evidence: ValidateDocument only handles properties at the top level
It doesn't recursively validate nested objects in properties
plan: Refactor ValidateDocument/validateField to be recursive
Support SchemaObject validation within validateField
notes: Implemented recursive validation with depth tracking
Added SchemaObject support in validateField for nested properties
Added array of objects validation support
Implemented depth limiting (max 10 levels) to prevent stack overflow
Added RawObject field to FieldValue and NewObjectFieldValue helper
Comprehensive test coverage: 7 test functions with 20+ subtests
All CI gates pass: make ci clean

tasks.title: BlockedResourceOther in PlaywrightFetcher
evidence: isBlockedType in playwright_fetcher.go doesn't handle BlockedResourceOther
This makes it impossible to block arbitrary resource types in Playwright
plan: Update isBlockedType to handle BlockedResourceOther
Map it to appropriate Playwright resource types or handle as fallback
notes: Added BlockedResourceOther case to isBlockedType function
Maps to non-essential Playwright resource types (script, xhr, fetch, websocket, eventsource, manifest, texttrack, other)
Added comprehensive test coverage with 28 test cases
Handles literal Playwright 'other' type for beacons, CSP reports, pings
Preserves existing behavior for specific blocked types (image, media, font, stylesheet)

tasks.title: Refactor CLI to reduce duplication
evidence: runScrape, runCrawl, and runResearch have high code duplication for flag parsing and job enqueuing
plan: Extract common flag sets and job enqueuing logic into helper functions
Simplify subcommand implementations
notes: Refactored runScrape, runCrawl, runResearch to use common helpers (registerCommonFlags, resolveAuthFromFlags, initJobManager, handleJobResult)
Refactored runServer to use initJobManager helper (missed in Phase 2)
Refactored runSchedule add to use registerBrowserFlags and registerPipelineFlags
Reduced ~230 lines of duplicated code across CLI commands
All tests pass, CI gate successful

tasks.title: Web UI: Handle multi-line results
evidence: loadResults in App.tsx only parses the first line of the results file
This hides results for crawl and research jobs which produce multiple lines
plan: Modify loadResults to parse all lines of the JSONL file
Update the UI to display a list of results with a details view for each
notes: Modified loadResults to parse all JSONL lines instead of just first line
Added ResultItem union type with CrawlResultItem and ResearchResultItem
Added resultItems state and selectedResultIndex for multi-result navigation
Added isCrawlResultItem and isResearchResultItem type guards
Fixed useEffect to clear stale display state when resultItems is empty
Fixed isResearchResultItem to explicitly check not a crawl result first
Fixed NormalizedView to check if item is crawl result before accessing normalized
Fixed ClusterItem.evidence to use EvidenceItem type consistently

tasks.title: Web UI: Add Cancel Job button
evidence: There is no way to cancel a running job from the Web UI
plan: Add a 'Cancel' button to job items in the UI
Implement the cancel call using the generated API client
notes: Implemented cancelJob function with deleteV1JobsById API call
Cancel button renders for queued/running jobs only
Proper error handling with user feedback
Added 'canceled' case to statusClass for visual feedback
All CI checks pass (generate, format, type-check, lint, build, test)

tasks.title: Web UI: Add Delete Job button
evidence: Jobs cannot be deleted from the system via the Web UI
plan: Ensure API supports job deletion (looks like it does in handleJob)
Add a 'Delete' button to the UI with a confirmation dialog
notes: Added DELETE /v1/jobs/{id}?force=true endpoint
Store.Delete() method with full test coverage
UI Delete button with confirmation dialog
Proper state cleanup (clears selection if deleted)
All CI checks passing
